title: Long Now Foundation: Incorruptible by Design
summary: Eric takes the stage at the Long Now Foundation's Cowell Theater at Fort Mason in San Francisco for a sold-out evening talk moderated by Denise Hearn, Long Now's Director of Strategic Initiatives. The talk explores the central question of the book: how do you build organizations that remain true to their founding mission across decades and generations? Ries argues that corruption isn't a moral failing — it's a structural one, and that the solution lies in designing governance mechanisms that are genuinely incorruptible. The Long Now Foundation, famous for thinking on 10,000-year timescales, provides the perfect venue for this argument about building things that last.
blockquote: How do we architect accountability mechanisms that outlive their creators?
